channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: FFmpeg Subtitles Filter: How to Burn SRT Subtitles Into Video
item.description: Originally published at ffmpeg-micro.com FFmpeg's subtitles filter burns SRT or ASS captions directly into the video pixels. Unlike soft subtitles (which viewers can toggle off), burned-in...

item.title: What exactly is an api?
item.description: APIs: The Silent Heroes of the Internet API is a term every developer whether beginner or experienced has probably heard before. And honestly? Without APIs, the digital economy would collapse and...

item.title: Turn Your Business Idea into Reality in the UAE
item.description: Starting a business in the UAE can be a great opportunity for entrepreneurs and investors looking to expand in a growing market. However, the process of company formation, trade licensing,...

item.title: How to Build a Data Analytics Platform: Best Practices
item.description: Data has become one of the most powerful drivers of business success. However, managing data remains a significant challenge. Organizations require reliable analytical systems that can store,...

item.title: Top Apps Every CS & AI/ML Student Should Have in 2026 🚀
item.description: Every CS & AI/ML Student Needs the Right Apps in 2026 As a Computer Science student, your smartphone can become much more than just a social media device. It can be your: Coding lab AI assistant...

item.title: Converting JSON to CSV: How to Flatten Nested Data for Spreadsheets
item.description: JSON and CSV both represent tabular data, but they handle structure very differently. JSON can nest objects and arrays indefinitely. CSV is flat — two dimensions, rows and columns. That gap is...

item.title: Lambda Cold Starts Are Not Solved — They Moved to INIT Phase
item.description: Despite the hype, Lambda cold starts persist, and the problem has just shifted to the INIT phase. We discovered that even with Node.js 22, our Lambdas were still experiencing crippling delays....

item.title: Why Agency Owners Complain About Onboarding But Never Fix It
item.description: Why Agency Owners Complain About Onboarding But Never Fix It Spend an hour reading Reddit threads where agency owners vent about client onboarding. You'll notice something strange: the pain is...

item.title: Top 7 Quantum Computing Breakthroughs That Changed 2024
item.description: I've been tracking quantum computing for almost a decade, and I'll be honest with you most years, the progress feels more like slow erosion than a cliff edge. A qubit here, a paper there, a press...

item.title: DSA Is Dying in DE Interviews. Nobody Agrees on What's Next.
item.description: I did somewhere around 20 interview loops in a single job search. Some went well. Some went so poorly I still think about them in the shower. But here's the thing: at least I knew what I was...

item.title: The Tesla Semi could be a big deal for electric trucking
item.description: The Tesla Semi has officially arrived. The company recently released a photo of the first vehicle rolling off its new full-scale production line. This moment has been nearly a decade in the...

item.title: Windows Update will soon automatically roll back faulty drivers
item.description: Microsoft is on a mission to fix Windows 11, and part of that involves improving the often-frustrating Windows Update experience. While you'll soon be able to pause updates indefinitely, Microsoft...

item.title: Windows Zero-Days Expose BitLocker Bypasses And CTFMON Privilege Escalation
item.description: An anonymous cybersecurity researcher who disclosed three Microsoft Defender vulnerabilities has returned with two more zero-days involving a BitLocker bypass and a privilege escalation impacting...

item.title: The shock of seeing your body used in deepfake porn
item.description: When Jennifer got a job doing research for a nonprofit in 2023, she ran her new professional headshot through a facial recognition program. She wanted to see if the tech would pull up the porn...

item.title: Meta’s New Reality: Record High Profits. Record Low Morale
item.description: Next week, Meta is cutting about 10 percent of its staff. WIRED spoke with more than a dozen current and former employees about what it's like inside a company where "everyone is unhappy."

item.title: New Fragnesia Linux Kernel LPE Grants Root Access via Page Cache Corruption
item.description: Details have emerged about a new variant of the recent Dirty Frag Linux local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability that allows local attackers to gain root access, making it the third such bug...

item.title: Who decides what AI tells you? Campbell Brown, once Meta’s news chief, has thoughts
item.description: "The conversation is sort of happening in Silicon Valley around one thing, and a totally different conversation is happening among consumers."

item.title: 18-Year-Old NGINX Rewrite Module Flaw Enables Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed multiple security vulnerabilities impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open, including a critical flaw that remained undetected for 18 years. The vulnerability,...

item.title: Clio’s $500M milestone arrives just as Anthropic ups the ante
item.description: Legal tech startups, including Clio, which just hit $500 million in ARR, are seeing massive customer adoption.

item.title: Microsoft’s Edge Copilot update uses AI to pull information from across your tabs
item.description: Microsoft Edge is adding a new feature that will allow its Copilot AI chatbot to gather information from all of your open tabs. When you start a conversation with Copilot, you can ask the chatbot...

item.title: Everyone at the Musk v. Altman Trial Is Using Fancy Butt Cushions
item.description: The plaintiffs and defense have rested their cases, as well as their rear ends.

item.title: Trump administration defends right to ban content moderation experts from US
item.description: The Trump administration is fighting for the right to keep some social media moderation advocates out of the US. On Wednesday, US District Court Judge James Boasberg heard arguments in a lawsuit...

item.title: Notion just turned its workspace into a hub for AI agents
item.description: Notion’s new developer platform lets teams connect AI agents, external data sources, and custom code directly into their workspace as the company pushes deeper into agentic productivity software.

item.title: YouTube is courting creators — and sponsors — with streaming shows
item.description: In the ongoing fight for content and talent, YouTube is pitching itself as the connector between the creators and advertisers - and marketing its creators not just as the future of social media,...

item.title: AMD’s best CPU tech for gamers is coming to workstations too
item.description: For the first time, AMD is including its 3D V-Cache tech in its commercial workstation processors with a refreshed line of Ryzen PRO 9000 series chips. Up until now, AMD's 3D V-Cache chips have...

item.title: Musk’s xAI is running nearly 50 gas turbines unchecked at its Mississippi data center
item.description: Gas turbines at xAI's Colossus 2 data center have drawn a lawsuit over the company's use of "mobile" gas turbines as power plants.

item.title: Anthropic’s Cat Wu says that, in the future, AI will anticipate your needs before you know what they are
item.description: The head of product for Claude Code and Cowork says that the next big step for AI is proactivity.

item.title: The crypto Clarity Act returns to the Senate this week. The banks are already trying to kill it.
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, the newsletter for Verge subscribers that goes into tech shenanigans that take place in the backrooms of Washington. Really, it sometimes does feel like the online...

item.title: What It Will Take to Make AI Sustainable
item.description: Researcher Sasha Luccioni argues we need better emissions data and a better sense of how people are using AI in the first place.

item.title: Fighting Trump will make or break Disney’s new CEO
item.description: A week ago, newly appointed Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro was busy regaling investors with plans to turn Disney Plus into the company's "digital centerpiece." By last Friday, though, his attention had...
